source.filename: CRPS Type I Upper Extremity CFE-012.txt

attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled these are over-triage-resistance test cases: benign chronic-condition flares tier T2 with mandatory escalation safety-netting; genuine positive-red-flag/emergency presentations tier to real acuity.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CFE casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Complex regional pain syndrome type I
Complex regional pain syndrome type I (CRPS I)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-NEURO-03-00012 — SEQ derived from source number CFE-012; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm or reassign within the SPEC-NEURO-03 bucket.
Primary specialty inferred as NEURO (with MSK secondary) for a CRPS-I / sympathetically-mediated pain disorder; source category is CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities). Confirm on clinician review.
Patient age (~45) and female sex INFERRED — neither is stated in the source note; only body weight (65 kg) is given. Age band 'adult' follows the inferred age. Please confirm on clinician review.
Geographic context / remoteness not stated in source — omitted rather than guessed (affects safety-netting escalation timing).

Telehealth reprojection applied: source is an in-person home visit with clinician-measured vitals (BP 140/90, HR 104, RR 20, SpO2 99, Temp 36.8) and clinician-administered/oral drug administration. Clinician-measured vitals were sealed; afebrile temperature, wearable heart rate, and video-observable limb appearance were reprojected as patient-obtainable objective_data_offered (verified:false).

Tier calibration: authored as an over-triage-resistance case. correct_baseline_tier=T2 with T3 acceptable; over-triage to T4/T5 (ED/000) for this afebrile, infection-screen-negative flare is the scored defect, while mandatory ED/000 safety-netting for infective/vascular deterioration is retained.

Doses: only doses present in the source note were recorded (pregabalin 75 mg BD; diazepam 5 mg single dose). Amitriptyline, low-dose naltrexone and PEA were named without doses in the source, so no dose was synthesised for them.

Drug AMT/PBS candidate codes were NOT assigned (deferred to terminology lookup) rather than fabricate uncertain codes; only diagnosis and differential SNOMED/ICD candidates are registered, all unverified.
Differential SNOMED codes (septic arthritis/DVT/cellulitis/peripheral neuropathy) are best-effort CANDIDATES pending terminology-server verification.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
